up.e: The bird flew up to the tree.

us.e: Let's all go to the park with us.

use.e: I use a pencil to draw pictures.

used.d: something that has been had or done before
used.e: I played with my used toys today.

under.d: Below or lower than something else
under.e: The cat is sleeping under the table.

until.e: We played outside until it got dark.

using.e: I'm using my pencil to draw a picture.

united.e: The team worked united to win the game.

understand.d: To know the meaning of something
understand.e: I understand how to solve this math problem.

university.d: A school where people study for degrees
university.e: My sister goes to the university in our city.

upon.d: On top of or higher than something
upon.e: The cat jumped up upon the table.

usually.e: I usually eat breakfast before going to school.

union.d: When people join together for a common goal
union.e: The workers formed a union to get better pay.

uk.e: Many people visit the UK to see the famous landmarks

unless.e: I can't go to the park unless I finish my homework.

unit.e: Each classroom is a unit of the whole school.

uses.d: Ways that something can be helpful or helpful
uses.e: The scientist uses many tools in her lab.

understanding.d: The ability to know and comprehend something
understanding.e: With practice, I gained a better understanding of how to solve the math problems.

users.e: The users of the app think it is easy to use.

un.d: A word part that means 'not' or 'the opposite of'
un.e: The student was unhappy when she didn't pass the test.

units.d: Smaller parts that make up a larger whole
units.e: The math class learned about different units of measurement.

useful.d: Something that is helpful and good to have
useful.e: A hammer is a useful tool for building things.

user.d: A person who uses something, like a computer or app
user.e: The app is easy for users to understand and use.

usa.d: The country where you live, made up of 50 states.
usa.e: The USA is a diverse country with many different cultures.

unique.d: One of a kind, the only one like it.
unique.e: My new toy is unique and special.

upper.d: the higher or top part of something
upper.e: She lives in the upper floor of the building.

urban.d: Relating to a city or town
urban.e: There are many tall buildings in the urban area.

update.d: To change something to make it more current or better.
update.e: I need to update the software on my computer.

unable.e: The dog was unable to climb over the fence.

understood.d: When you know and accept what something means
understood.e: I understood the directions the teacher gave for the activity.

unfortunately.d: Sadly, or in an unhappy way.
unfortunately.e: Unfortunately, the park was closed due to rain.

universe.d: All of the stars, planets, and other things in space.
universe.e: The universe is full of millions of galaxies.

updated.d: When something is made new or modern.
updated.e: My phone was updated to have a newer and better design.

usual.d: Something that happens or is done often.

upset.d: Feeling unhappy, worried, or angry about something.
upset.e: The child was upset when they lost their favorite toy.

unknown.d: Something that is not known or recognized
unknown.e: The explorer discovered an unknown island.

ultimately.d: In the end, after everything is done.
ultimately.e: Ultimately, the team won the game.

unlike.d: Different from or not the same as something else.
unlike.e: The new puppy is unlike my old dog.

universal.d: something that is the same for everyone
universal.e: Kindness is a universal value that everyone should have.

ultimate.e: The team's ultimate goal is to win the championship.

uncle.e: My uncle takes me to the park on Saturdays.

unusual.d: Something that is not normal or common.
unusual.e: The butterfly with the unusual colors landed on the flower.
